All right, JB in the house, time for your daily motivation slash skills training. Listen, I love this topic we're on for this week. All right. So this is now week seven for straight line certification. Again to those who are not on it, you are missing a very, very serious bow here. All right. The results people are getting this from this all the charts. This particular module vision focus, right? I have to say without having the ability to create, I mean to really have in your mind a vision future that literally turns you on, makes you want to jump out of bed in the morning and sees every day without that, that clear target to aim for. And then also understanding the process that I teach in this module, bifurcated focus. How you keep yourself zoomed in on that vision with laser guided precision, but you don't have tunnel vision. That's the distinction. Tunnel vision means that's my vision. I'm focused on it. I don't care what comes up along the way. I am doing XYZ only XYZ and that's that. That's tunnel vision. That's a disaster. Let me tell you why. Your vision is a living breathing organism. You've heard me say that in certification, right? What I mean by that is it's constantly growing, evolving, changing, getting better. In other words, what happens as you move through life, as you're going for the brass ring, so to speak, as you're looking to hit your vision, that's your target. You're looking to hit it. You want to hit your target, right? You have these goals along the way. You have short term daily goals. You have weekly goals, monthly, annual goals, right? So short and long term goals. Your goals serve as your signposts along the way. There's short term indicators that say whether I'm moving in the right direction or the wrong direction. Am I moving towards my vision or am I moving away from my vision? That's what your goals are for, right? Now remember, you're not always going to hit your goals. It's not going to happen because we set lofty goals. We want to stretch, all right? So you have these goals. You have the vision sitting atop the goals. That's your long term target, right? But as you're out there moving through the world, you know, trying to hit these daily and weekly and monthly goals, other opportunities come up along the way. They're there. And what happens is if you are so focused in on your vision, anything that doesn't directly relate to that will simply be ignored. Remember, we only see what we allow ourselves to see. If we block the other opportunities, they just fly over our head. There's opportunity all around you all the time. The problem is you're just not noticing it because either you have limiting beliefs. We'll get to that later on. Or you're so locked into one way of thinking, into one outcome, to one vision that you're like, you know what? That's not for me. It's not what I'm doing right now. That's not how you succeed in life. I could go on and on and on about every company out there that started off with one vision and then was heading towards that. They were doing okay. Maybe, maybe not. Maybe doing real. But then something came into the purview of the CEO or someone that was very smart that worked this company and like, ooh, that's not quite what we do, but it kind of would be interesting. And they let's integrate that. Let's give it a test. See if it works integrated into our vision. If it does and bam, they soar to massive success. I'm going to give you an example. It's not the best example here because the company's stupid as shit. Okay. Because this is example, you know, Groupon, right? Groupon had this stupid business model, a stupid business model that they were going to go do crowdsourcing for certain projects to try to like, okay, let's build a dome around the city of Chicago. I'm serious. I'm not even kidding you. Like a dome around the whole city of Chicago. And let's see if we can raise money through crowdsourcing. And by saying like, you know, unless we get the X number of dollars, we won't release the money. So incentivize people to call their friends. That's how Groupon got started, but not in the sense of daily deals. And they were just, you know, floundering because it's kind of a silly concept. But there was something to it. Then when we have to pay the bills, why don't we just like try to do something like locally for like a nail sign. I don't know exactly what it's about for a nail. So let's just try something like for a little company that, and they try this little daily deal thing. Okay. Just kind of pay the bills and all of a sudden, well, holy Christ. I'm like, whoa, and everybody signs up. And what do they do? They pivoted, they integrated that. And that became the vision of the company and the mission.
